description: trust set — Submit TrustSet transactions to create, modify, or remove XRPL trust lines from the CLI

userStories.title: trust set core command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create or update a trust line so I can hold issued tokens from a specific issuer.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/trust.ts with a trustCommand (new Command('trust')) containing a 'set' subcommand (alias 's')
Register trustCommand in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts

Required options: --currency <code> (3-char ASCII auto-uppercased, or 40-char hex; exits 1 with descriptive error on invalid format), --issuer <address-or-alias> (resolved via resolveAccount()), --limit <value> (decimal string; '0' removes the trust line)

Key material — exactly one required: --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, --account <address-or-alias> (loads from keystore; --password skips interactive prompt with insecure warning to stderr; --keystore <dir> and XRPL_KEYSTORE env var respected)
Exits 1 with error if zero or more than one key material option is provided
Global --node / -n respected via getNodeUrl(cmd)
Builds TrustSet tx: { TransactionType: 'TrustSet', Account: <signer-address>, LimitAmount: { currency, issuer, value } }
Calls client.autofill(tx), signs with wallet.sign(), then client.submitAndWait() by default
Human-readable success output: 'Transaction: <hash>', 'Result:      <result-code>', 'Fee:         <fee-xrp> XRP', 'Ledger:      <ledger-index>'
--no-wait: calls client.submit() instead; prints only 'Transaction: <hash>'
--json: outputs { hash, result, fee, ledger } or { hash } with --no-wait
--dry-run: prints { tx_blob, tx: <autofilled-tx-object> } as JSON to stdout without submitting; exits 0
Exits 1 on tec*/tef*/tem* result codes with error to stderr
Catches TimeoutError and exits 1 with 'Error: transaction expired (LastLedgerSequence exceeded)'
E2E test: fund a testnet account from faucet, run 'trust set --currency USD --issuer <that-address> --limit 1000 --seed <seed>', assert exit 0 and stdout contains 'tesSUCCESS'
E2E test: --dry-run outputs JSON with TransactionType 'TrustSet' and does not submit (query account trust-lines after and assert no new trust line)
E2E test: --no-wait exits 0 and stdout contains a 64-char hex hash
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: TrustSet flags and quality options
userStories.description: As a user, I want to control NoRipple, Freeze, and Auth flags on a trust line so I can configure rippling and compliance behaviour.
acceptanceCriteria: --no-ripple flag: adds TrustSetFlags.tfSetNoRipple (0x00020000) to Flags via bitwise OR
--clear-no-ripple flag: adds TrustSetFlags.tfClearNoRipple (0x00040000) to Flags
--freeze flag: adds TrustSetFlags.tfSetFreeze (0x00100000) to Flags
--unfreeze flag: adds TrustSetFlags.tfClearFreeze (0x00200000) to Flags
--auth flag: adds TrustSetFlags.tfSetfAuth (0x00010000) to Flags
Multiple flags combine via bitwise OR into a single Flags field on the tx
--quality-in <n>: sets QualityIn field (unsigned integer); only added to tx when explicitly provided
--quality-out <n>: sets QualityOut field (unsigned integer); only added to tx when explicitly provided
Exits 1 with error to stderr if both --no-ripple and --clear-no-ripple are passed
Exits 1 with error to stderr if both --freeze and --unfreeze are passed

E2E test: run 'trust set --currency USD --issuer <addr> --limit 1000 --no-ripple --seed <seed>', then query account_lines via 'account trust-lines --json', assert the trust line entry has no_ripple: true
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
